description: NGW Camera Body Database — covers current and recent-gen mirrorless, DSLR, and medium format bodies relevant to portrait, editorial, and studio photography.

_field_guide.max_sync_speed: Integer denominator of 1/X sync speed (e.g. 250 = 1/250s). HSS-capable bodies listed at native sync.
_field_guide.has_hss: High Speed Sync or Electronic Front Curtain Shutter that allows sync beyond native speed
_field_guide.best_for: Portrait-relevant use cases the body excels at
_field_guide.flash_system: Describes native/built-in radio TTL or sync port compatibility
_field_guide.autofocus_class: pro | advanced | standard — based on subject tracking in portrait scenarios

cameras.flash_system: Standard hot shoe (PC sync port). Full TTL + HSS with Sony flashes and compatible third-party (Godox, Profoto).
cameras.notes: 61 MP demands sharp lenses, precise focus, and vibration-free triggering. AI subject recognition tracks eyes even in complex editorial compositions. Best high-res portrait body from Sony.

cameras.flash_system: Standard hot shoe (PC sync port). Full TTL + HSS with Sony, Godox, Profoto Air TTL.
cameras.notes: 33 MP sweet spot — enough resolution for editorial print, enough headroom for ISO 6400 portraits. The all-rounder for working portrait photographers.

cameras.flash_system: Standard hot shoe (no PC sync). Full TTL + HSS with Sony, Godox, Profoto Air TTL.
cameras.notes: Still a workhorse value body. Excellent low-light performance, reliable eye-AF. Dynamic range handles mixed-light scenarios well. No PC sync port requires adapter for pack systems.

cameras.flash_system: Standard hot shoe (PC sync). Global shutter enables full-speed sync at any shutter speed — no HSS needed. Compatible with all flash systems.
cameras.notes: Global shutter eliminates rolling shutter and sync limitations. Can fire strobes at 1/40000s in bright sun without ND filters. Revolutionary for outdoor editorial flash work. Dynamic range slightly below BSI-CMOS peers — shadows slightly noisier.

cameras.flash_system: Standard hot shoe. Full flash compatible but body prioritizes continuous light. Best for LED/HMI panel setups.
cameras.notes: Cinema body designed for continuous light and video. Base ISO 800 means continuous LED rigs perform better than on stills bodies. Used heavily in fashion video and look-book shoots.

cameras.flash_system: Standard hot shoe (no PC sync). Full TTL + HSS with Canon Speedlite, Profoto, Godox. E-TTL II radio built-in for Canon RT system.
cameras.notes: 45 MP with IBIS and Canon's best-ever eye-AF. The go-to for Canon photographers doing high-end portrait and editorial work. Excellent flash sync reliability.

cameras.flash_system: Standard hot shoe (no PC sync). Full TTL + HSS with Canon RT radio system natively. Godox and Profoto Air TTL compatible.
cameras.notes: 24 MP resolution frees headroom for incredible ISO performance. Best Sony A7 IV competitor. Canon colour science widely praised for skin tones — warm and natural straight from camera.

cameras.flash_system: Standard hot shoe (PC sync port). Full Canon RT radio TTL system built in. Pro-grade reliability.
cameras.notes: Stacked CMOS for 30 fps. Eye-controlled AF is unique to Canon. Best portrait flash work is with E-TTL radio system at high frame rates for run-and-gun editorial. PC sync port is welcome at this price.

cameras.flash_system: Standard hot shoe (PC sync port). Full TTL + HSS with Nikon SB/Speedlight, Profoto Air TTL, Godox. PC sync port for pack-and-head systems.
cameras.notes: Z9 performance in a lighter body. 46 MP at base ISO 64 gives exceptional dynamic range — ideal for controlled studio work where exposure latitude matters. Best Nikon for portrait work.

cameras.flash_system: Standard hot shoe (PC sync port). No mechanical shutter — electronic only. Full HSS without need for mechanical shutter.
cameras.notes: Stacked CMOS with no mechanical shutter means electronic sync at full speed. 46 MP + stacked sensor + 120 fps crop mode. The professional all-rounder. Electronic shutter allows quiet continuous shooting during studio sessions.

cameras.flash_system: Standard hot shoe (no PC sync). Full TTL + HSS with Nikon and third-party systems. Retro body with modern capability.
cameras.notes: Retro form factor with Z6 III sensor. Excellent for portrait work where a less intimidating camera puts subjects at ease. No PC sync is the only limitation for pack systems — use adapter.

cameras.flash_system: Standard hot shoe (no PC sync). Full TTL + HSS. Compatible with Godox, Profoto Air, Nikon SB system.
cameras.notes: Partially stacked CMOS — 6K video and 120 fps for portraits. Best value full-frame mirrorless from Nikon. Excellent for working photographers who need stills and video from one body.

cameras.flash_system: Standard hot shoe (no PC sync). Full TTL + HSS with Fujifilm EF-X series, Godox, Profoto Air TTL.
cameras.notes: 40 MP APS-C sensor is exceptional for print work. Film simulations (Classic Neg, Velvia, Eterna) are workflow-changing for editorial photographers. APS-C crop means 1.5× effective focal length — a 56mm f/1.2 renders like 85mm f/1.8 for portraits.

cameras.flash_system: Standard hot shoe. Lower max sync speed (1/125s) requires careful exposure management with fast strobes. PC sync port available.
cameras.notes: 102 MP medium format sensor delivers tonal gradations impossible to replicate with 35mm sensors. Skin tones at base ISO are the gold standard for beauty advertising. Larger sensor requires adjusted lighting setups — illuminance falls off faster across the image plane.

cameras.flash_system: Standard hot shoe (PC sync). Same sync speed limitation as GFX 100S II — plan around 1/125s for mixed ambient/strobe.
cameras.notes: Entry point into Fujifilm medium format at a compelling price. 51 MP is more forgiving than 102 MP for focus accuracy and storage. The depth of field and micro-contrast still unmistakably medium format.

cameras.flash_system: Standard hot shoe (PC sync). Hasselblad XH sync adapter available. Lower sync speed requires ND filters outdoors or HSS-capable strobes.
cameras.notes: 100 MP 44×33mm BSI sensor with industry-leading 15.1 EV dynamic range. The benchmark for tonal separation in skin. Leaf shutter XCD lenses allow up to 1/2000s sync. Price point limits it to top-tier commercial work.

cameras.flash_system: Built-in leaf shutter allows flash sync up to 1/4000s — no HSS needed outdoors. Hot shoe compatible with standard TTL flashes.
cameras.notes: Leaf shutter is the hidden superpower for outdoor flash — sync at 1/4000s in broad daylight without ND filter or HSS. 40 MP Fujifilm sensor with film simulations. The ideal travel editorial camera with serious strobe capability.

cameras.flash_system: Standard hot shoe (no PC sync, no card slot). Full TTL + HSS with Canon RT radio system. Budget entry to pro flash workflow.
cameras.notes: Most affordable full-frame Canon mirrorless. No IBIS, no dual cards, no PC sync — but full-frame sensor and Canon colour science are all there. Best entry point for portrait photographers starting with studio flash.

cameras.flash_system: Standard hot shoe (PC sync port). Leica Multifunction-Shoe. Compatible with all standard flash systems. Profoto and Godox air TTL via dedicated triggers.
cameras.notes: 60 MP at base ISO 64 with the highest dynamic range of any 35mm body. Colour rendering is Leica-distinct — desaturated midtones, precise skin neutrality. Used extensively in European fashion editorial.

cameras.flash_system: Standard hot shoe. Compatible with Olympus and Metz TTL. Godox TTL via X trigger. Best used with fill flash in daylight.
cameras.notes: Micro four-thirds means 2× crop — 50mm lens = 100mm portrait equivalent. IBIS is the best in class at 8.5 stops. Weathersealing allows shooting in rain. MFT sensor limits low-light performance but the body's feature set for location work is exceptional.

cameras.flash_system: Standard hot shoe (PC sync port). Full TTL + HSS with Nikon SB-series, Godox, Profoto Air TTL. Excellent pack-and-head sync via PC port.
cameras.notes: Still the benchmark DSLR for portrait work. 46 MP at base ISO 64 with 14.8 EV dynamic range. Optical viewfinder preferred by photographers who need 100% of the scene. No eye-tracking AF but 153-point AF is excellent for directed portraits.

cameras.flash_system: Standard hot shoe (PC sync port). Full E-TTL II + HSS. Compatible with Canon RT radio system, Godox, Profoto Air TTL.
cameras.notes: Millions still in active use. Canon's colour rendering for skin remains best-in-class for warm natural tones. OVF and ergonomics make it a preferred working tool for event photographers who transitioned from 5D III.

cameras.flash_system: Standard hot shoe (no PC sync). Full TTL + HSS with Sony, Godox, Profoto Air TTL. Best APS-C body for flash work from Sony.
cameras.notes: Sony's current APS-C flagship. AI subject recognition inherited from A7R V. 1.5× crop means 35mm reads as 52mm, 50mm as 75mm — a portrait-friendly effective focal length shift. Best APS-C option for photographers in Sony ecosystem.

cameras.flash_system: Standard hot shoe. Full TTL + HSS with Panasonic and compatible third-party flashes. Phase detection AF now available — significant improvement over S5 I.
cameras.notes: Panasonic's first L-mount body with phase detect AF. The go-to for video-heavy photographers who also shoot portraits. V-Log and 6K video. Skin tones slightly cooler than Canon/Nikon — may require warming in post for warm editorial looks.

cameras.flash_system: Standard hot shoe (no PC sync). Full TTL + HSS with Fujifilm EF-X, Godox, Profoto Air TTL. 40 MP APS-C = 61 MP equivalent in capture surface density.
cameras.notes: 40 MP APS-C is pixel-for-pixel competitive with Sony A7R IV at 61 MP for crop-in flexibility. 8K video. Fujifilm film simulations add a workflow advantage for editorial photographers seeking specific looks without heavy grading.

cameras.flash_system: Standard hot shoe. Full TTL + HSS with Fujifilm EF-X, Godox, Profoto Air TTL.
cameras.notes: Entry-point into Fujifilm mirrorless with film simulations and IBIS. Vari-angle screen makes it excellent for self-shooting content creators doing portrait-style videos and photos.

cameras.flash_system: Standard hot shoe. Full TTL + HSS with Nikon and Godox TTL.
cameras.notes: Affordable entry to Nikon Z system with APS-C crop. Subject tracking is significantly improved from Z50. A good first body for portrait photographers building toward a full-frame upgrade path in the Z mount ecosystem.

cameras.flash_system: Standard hot shoe. Full TTL + HSS with Canon RT system. Excellent entry into Canon flash ecosystem — all RT triggers work.
cameras.notes: Canon's entry APS-C mirrorless with Dual Pixel CMOS AF. Subject eye tracking is reliable. Great for portrait photographers starting out — pairs well with the RF 50mm f/1.8 for a sharp, affordable portrait kit at 80mm equivalent.

cameras.flash_system: Standard hot shoe. Built-in leaf shutter in the Summilux 28mm f/1.7 ASPH allows up to 1/2000s sync — exceptional outdoor flash capability. Leica M-shoe compatible accessories.
cameras.notes: Fixed 28mm f/1.7 lens. Leaf shutter syncs to 1/2000s for outdoor editorial flash without ND or HSS. The camera for photographers who want a single premium tool that does everything beautifully. Environmental and documentary portraiture.

cameras.flash_system: Leaf shutter in XF/XT lenses allows sync up to 1/1600s. Dedicated Profoto integration. Capture One software is the industry standard for Phase One tethered studio work.
cameras.notes: The 150 MP IQ4 back on XF or XT body is the highest-resolution system available for commercial photography. 3 frame per second max. Capture One color profiles are tuned specifically to the Phase One sensor — result is unmatched tonal accuracy for skin. Limited to controlled studio environments due to size, weight, and cost.
